fix: Install: npm install next-pwa (or @ducanh2912/next-pwa for App Router support)
Configure next.config.js: const withPWA = require('next-pwa')({ dest: 'public', disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' }); module.exports = withPWA(nextConfig)
Create public/manifest.json: { name, short_name, icons: [{src, sizes, type}], start_url: '/', display: 'standalone', theme_color, background_color }
Add to layout.tsx: <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json' /> <meta name='theme-color' content='#...' />
Service worker caches pages and assets for offline access
For custom caching: configure runtimeCaching in next-pwa config
Limitation on Vercel: service worker works for static assets; API routes still need network

root_cause: High TTFB indicates slow server-side processing before sending the first byte to client
fix: Check database query time: add timing logs around DB calls
Enable streaming: return partial HTML immediately with Suspense boundaries
Reduce computation: cache expensive calculations in Redis
Regional optimization: deploy functions in same region as database
Connection reuse: ensure Prisma client is singleton (not recreated per request)
Reduce middleware overhead: skip heavy checks for public/static routes
Use ISR/static generation for pages that don't need per-request data

root_cause: High INP indicates event handlers taking too long, blocking the main thread
fix: Break long tasks: use startTransition for non-urgent state updates
Debounce frequent events: search input, scroll handlers, resize events
Move heavy computation to Web Workers or server (Server Actions)
Use CSS for animations (transform, opacity) instead of JS-driven animations
Virtualize long lists: use react-window or tanstack-virtual for 100+ items
Defer non-critical updates: useTransition + useDeferredValue for background updates
Profile with Chrome DevTools > Performance to identify long tasks

root_cause: Prisma enum values out of sync with database enum after schema change
fix: Run migration: npx prisma migrate dev --name update-enum-values
For existing data: create migration that alters the enum type in SQL
PostgreSQL enum alteration: ALTER TYPE "Role" ADD VALUE 'MODERATOR'
Cannot remove enum values easily in PostgreSQL - create new enum, migrate data, drop old
After schema change: npx prisma generate to update client types
Use String type instead of enum for frequently changing value sets

root_cause: Function execution exceeded the maximum allowed duration for the Vercel plan
fix: Hobby plan: 10s (60s with maxDuration), Pro: 60s (300s with maxDuration), Enterprise: 900s
Set maxDuration: export const maxDuration = 300 // in route.ts (requires Pro plan for >60s)
Break into smaller operations: split large jobs into multiple API calls or use Inngest steps
Use streaming for long responses: return partial data immediately with ReadableStream
Offload to background: QStash/Inngest for processing, return 202 Accepted immediately
Optimize: add database indexes, cache results, reduce payload size

root_cause: Metadata not refreshing on client-side navigation between pages
fix: Ensure metadata is exported from the correct page.tsx or layout.tsx (not a client component)
Use generateMetadata for dynamic metadata - it runs on every navigation
Don't put metadata in 'use client' components - it must be in Server Components
Layout metadata is inherited by children - page metadata overrides layout metadata
For template patterns: use title.template in layout, title string in pages
Clear browser cache and verify in View Source (not DevTools Elements which shows JS-modified DOM)

symptoms: cannot use headers/cookies during static generation
root_cause: Using dynamic APIs (cookies, headers, searchParams) in a page that Next.js tries to statically generate
fix: Add dynamic force: export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' at top of page/layout
Or use: export const revalidate = 0 to opt out of static generation
Understand: pages using cookies(), headers(), or searchParams are automatically dynamic
If you don't need dynamic: refactor to avoid cookies/headers in that component
Move dynamic logic to a client component or a separate Server Component with Suspense
Check: next build output shows which pages are static vs dynamic

root_cause: useSearchParams causes hydration mismatch because search params differ between server and client
fix: Wrap component using useSearchParams in Suspense: <Suspense fallback={null}><SearchComponent /></Suspense>
This is required in Next.js App Router - useSearchParams opts into client-side rendering
Without Suspense boundary: entire page becomes client-rendered (bad for SEO)
For server-side access: use page props searchParams (in page.tsx) instead of the hook
Pattern: page.tsx receives searchParams prop → passes to client component → component uses for interaction
The Suspense boundary allows the rest of the page to remain server-rendered

symptoms: React key warning with dynamic content
root_cause: React reuses component instances when key doesn't change, causing stale state
fix: Force remount with unique key: <Component key={entityId} /> resets all state when ID changes
Common issue: form doesn't reset when navigating to edit different item (same route, different params)
Fix: add key={params.id} to the form component in parent
Alternative: use useEffect to reset state when param changes (less clean)
For lists: always use stable unique IDs as keys, never array indices for dynamic lists
Router-level: usePathname() or useParams() in key to force reset on navigation

root_cause: Custom hooks encapsulate reusable stateful logic and side effects
fix: useDebounce: delays updating value until user stops typing: const debouncedSearch = useDebounce(search, 300)
useLocalStorage: persistent state synced with localStorage: const [theme, setTheme] = useLocalStorage('theme', 'light')
useMediaQuery: responsive behavior in JS: const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)')
usePrevious: track previous value: const prevCount = usePrevious(count)
useClickOutside: detect clicks outside element (for dropdowns/modals)
Rules: hooks start with 'use', can call other hooks, can't be conditional, return [value, setter] or { state, actions }

root_cause: React Context should be used sparingly and structured to avoid unnecessary re-renders
fix: Use context for: theme, locale, auth session, feature flags (rarely changing values)
Don't use for: frequently updating state (use Zustand/Jotai or prop drilling instead)
Split contexts: AuthContext, ThemeContext, ToastContext (not one giant AppContext)
Memoize provider value: const value = useMemo(() => ({ user, logout }), [user])
For frequent updates: useContext causes re-render of ALL consumers on any value change
Pattern: colocate provider with the subtree that needs it, not at root
Alternative: use Server Components to pass data without context (no prop drilling needed)

root_cause: Zustand provides simple global state without Context boilerplate or re-render issues
fix: Install: npm install zustand
Create store: import { create } from 'zustand'; export const useStore = create<State>((set) => ({ count: 0, increment: () => set((s) => ({ count: s.count + 1 })) }))
Use in component: const count = useStore((s) => s.count) // only re-renders when count changes
Selectors prevent unnecessary re-renders: useStore(s => s.specificField)
Persist: import { persist } from 'zustand/middleware'; create(persist(storeConfig, { name: 'app-storage' }))
SSR-safe: Zustand works in both server and client components (no hydration issues with proper setup)

root_cause: Complete webhook verification pattern for receiving events from external services
fix: Stripe: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!)
QStash: const receiver = new Receiver({ currentSigningKey, nextSigningKey }); await receiver.verify({ signature, body })
Generic HMAC: const expected = crypto.createHmac('sha256', secret).update(rawBody).digest('hex'); if (sig !== expected) return 403
IMPORTANT: use raw body (not parsed JSON) for signature verification
In App Router: const body = await request.text() (not request.json()) for raw body
Return 200 quickly, process async: acknowledge receipt, queue for processing with Inngest/QStash
Log webhook events for debugging: store raw payload + headers in database

root_cause: Complete RBAC implementation with role checking in middleware, server components, and API routes
fix: Define roles: enum Role { USER, MODERATOR, ADMIN } - store in User model
Middleware check: const session = await getSession(); if (isAdminRoute(path) && session.user.role !== 'ADMIN') return NextResponse.redirect('/unauthorized')
Server Component: const user = await getUser(); if (user.role !== 'ADMIN') redirect('/unauthorized')
API route: export async function DELETE(req) { const session = await auth(); if (session.user.role !== 'ADMIN') return Response.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 }) }
UI: conditionally render admin features: {user.role === 'ADMIN' && <AdminPanel />}
Always check server-side (never trust client-only checks for security)
For complex permissions: use CASL or similar library for fine-grained access control

root_cause: Complete implementation for exporting data as downloadable CSV file
fix: API route: app/api/export/route.ts

Generate CSV: const headers = ['Name', 'Email', 'Created']; const rows = data.map(d => [d.name, d.email, d.createdAt.toISOString()]); const csv = [headers, ...rows].map(r => r.map(c => `"${c}"`).join(',')).join('\n')
Return as download: return new Response(csv, { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/csv', 'Content-Disposition': 'attachment; filename="export.csv"' } })
Client trigger: const res = await fetch('/api/export'); const blob = await res.blob(); const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob); window.open(url)
For large exports: stream CSV rows to avoid memory issues
Alternative: generate in Inngest background job, store in Vercel Blob, email download link

root_cause: Complete multi-step form with per-step validation, navigation, and state persistence
fix: State: const [step, setStep] = useState(1); const [formData, setFormData] = useState<Partial<FormData>>({})
Per-step Zod schemas: const Step1Schema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(2) }); const Step2Schema = z.object({ email: z.string().email() })
Validate on Next: const result = StepSchemas[step].safeParse(formData); if (!result.success) setErrors(result.error.flatten())
Merge data: setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, ...stepData })); setStep(step + 1)
Submit all on final step: await submitForm(formData) // send complete data to server action
Persist progress: store in sessionStorage or Redis for recovery across sessions
Progress indicator: <Stepper currentStep={step} steps={['Info', 'Contact', 'Review']} />

root_cause: Complete in-app notification system with real-time delivery and read tracking
fix: Database: model Notification { id, userId, type, title, body, isRead, createdAt }
Create notification: await prisma.notification.create({ data: { userId, type: 'comment', title: 'New comment on your post' } })
Real-time delivery with Supabase: subscribe to notifications table filtered by userId
Unread count: const count = await prisma.notification.count({ where: { userId, isRead: false } })
Mark read: await prisma.notification.updateMany({ where: { userId, isRead: false }, data: { isRead: true } })
Bell component: display count badge, dropdown with recent notifications, mark-all-read button
Cache count in Redis: redis.set(`notif:count:${userId}`, count, { ex: 60 }); invalidate on new notification

root_cause: Monorepo projects need specific Vercel configuration for correct build targeting
fix: Set Root Directory in Vercel: Project Settings > General > Root Directory → packages/web
Or use vercel.json in root: { "buildCommand": "cd ../.. && turbo run build --filter=web", "framework": "nextjs" }
Install command: npm install (from root, installs all workspaces)
Turborepo: vercel detects turbo.json and uses turbo for builds automatically
Environment variables: set at root level, available to all packages
Ignore build step: Settings > Git > Ignored Build Step → npx turbo-ignore (skip unchanged packages)

root_cause: Systematic approach to identifying and fixing slow API routes on Vercel
fix: 1. Add timing logs: const start = Date.now(); // after each operation: console.log(`DB query: ${Date.now()-start}ms`)
2. Check Vercel Functions tab: see duration, memory usage, cold start indicator
3. Common culprits in order: a) unindexed DB query b) N+1 queries c) large payload serialization d) cold start
4. Fix N+1: use include/join instead of loop queries: prisma.post.findMany({ include: { author: true } })
5. Fix large payload: select only needed fields, paginate results
6. Fix cold start: reduce bundle size, use edge runtime for simple routes
7. Add Redis cache for expensive computations: cache result with appropriate TTL

root_cause: Systematic approach to finding and fixing React hydration mismatches
fix: 1. Check error message: it tells you what text/attribute differs between server and client
2. Common causes: a) Date/time rendering without UTC b) browser extensions modifying DOM c) localStorage-dependent rendering d) window/document usage during SSR
3. Fix dates: use suppressHydrationWarning or format consistently with UTC
4. Fix browser-only code: wrap in useEffect or use dynamic(() => import('./Component'), { ssr: false })
5. Fix conditional rendering: if (typeof window !== 'undefined') → move to useEffect with useState
6. Verify: disable browser extensions to rule them out
7. Use React DevTools to identify the specific component causing the mismatch

root_cause: Strategic indexing dramatically improves query performance without over-indexing
fix: Index columns used in WHERE clauses: especially foreign keys and filter fields
Index columns used in ORDER BY: enables index-based sorting instead of filesort
Compound indexes: match your most common query pattern: @@index([userId, createdAt])
Don't over-index: each index slows down writes and uses storage
Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to verify index usage: queries should show 'Index Scan' not 'Seq Scan'
Partial indexes for filtered queries: CREATE INDEX idx ON posts(status) WHERE status = 'published'
Monitor with pg_stat_user_indexes to find unused indexes to remove

root_cause: Generics enable reusable, type-safe code without sacrificing type information
fix: Use when function works with multiple types: function first<T>(arr: T[]): T | undefined { return arr[0] }
Generic API wrapper: async function fetchApi<T>(url: string): Promise<ApiResponse<T>>
Generic React component: function List<T extends { id: string }>({ items, renderItem }: { items: T[], renderItem: (item: T) => ReactNode })
Constrain generics: <T extends Record<string, unknown>> instead of just <T>
Default generics: <T = string> for optional type parameters
Don't over-generify: if there's only one type, just use it directly

root_cause: Consistent commit messages enable automated changelogs and clear history
fix: Format: <type>(<scope>): <description> — e.g., feat(auth): add Google OAuth login
Types: feat (new feature), fix (bug fix), docs, style, refactor, perf, test, chore, ci
Keep subject under 72 characters, use imperative mood (add not added)
Body: explain WHY (not what - the diff shows what), reference issue numbers
Breaking changes: add ! after type: feat(api)!: change response format
Tools: commitlint + husky for enforcement, conventional-changelog for auto-changelog

root_cause: Composition patterns create flexible, reusable component APIs
fix: Compound components: <Card><Card.Header>Title</Card.Header><Card.Body>Content</Card.Body></Card>
Render props: <DataFetcher url='/api/users'>{(data) => <UserList users={data} />}</DataFetcher>
Slot pattern: <Layout header={<Nav />} sidebar={<Menu />}>{children}</Layout>
Prefer composition over configuration: multiple small components over one mega-component with many props
Children as function: enables parent to pass computed values to children
Use Radix UI primitives as composition base: headless, accessible, composable

root_cause: Validate data at every trust boundary: user input, API responses, database results, file reads
fix: User input → Server Action: validate with Zod schema, return typed errors to client
External API response: validate shape before using (APIs can change without notice)
URL parameters: z.object({ id: z.string().uuid() }).parse(params)
Webhook payload: verify signature THEN validate body schema
File content: validate format, size, MIME type server-side
Environment variables: validate at startup with zod: env.parse(process.env)
Never trust client-side validation alone: it's UX convenience, not security

root_cause: Modern form handling combines Server Actions with proper UX feedback
fix: Use Server Actions for form submission: <form action={submitAction}>
Show pending state: import { useFormStatus } from 'react-dom'; const { pending } = useFormStatus(); <button disabled={pending}>
Validate client-side for UX, server-side for security (both with same Zod schema)
Show field-level errors: return { fieldErrors } from server action, display per-input
Progressive enhancement: forms work without JavaScript (submit → server action → redirect)
Optimistic updates: useOptimistic for instant feedback while server processes
Reset form on success: use key prop on form or ref.reset()

root_cause: Users should never see raw errors or blank screens - always provide recovery options
fix: Show user-friendly message: 'Something went wrong' not 'TypeError: Cannot read property of undefined'
Provide recovery action: 'Try Again' button that calls reset() in error.tsx
Partial failure: if sidebar data fails, show page content with fallback sidebar
Offline handling: detect navigator.onLine, queue actions for retry when back online
Form errors: preserve user input, show field-specific errors, don't clear the form
Loading failure: show 'Loading failed' with retry, not infinite spinner

root_cause: TanStack Query provides powerful client-side data fetching with caching and synchronization
fix: Install: npm install @tanstack/react-query
Provider: create QueryClientProvider wrapper (must be 'use client')
Basic query: const { data, isLoading, error } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['posts'], queryFn: () => fetch('/api/posts').then(r => r.json()) })
Mutation: const mutation = useMutation({ mutationFn: createPost, onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'] }) })
With Server Components: prefetch on server, hydrate on client for instant display
Use for: paginated data, polling, optimistic updates, offline support, infinite scroll
Don't use for: initial page data (use Server Components instead)

root_cause: Need a toast notification system for showing transient messages to users
fix: Install: npm install sonner (lightweight, accessible toast library)
Add Toaster to layout: import { Toaster } from 'sonner'; <Toaster position='top-right' richColors />
Show toast: import { toast } from 'sonner'; toast.success('Post created!'); toast.error('Failed to save')
From Server Action: return { success: true, message: 'Created!' } → client shows toast based on result
With promise: toast.promise(saveData(), { loading: 'Saving...', success: 'Saved!', error: 'Failed' })
Customize: toast('Custom', { description: 'Details here', duration: 5000, action: { label: 'Undo', onClick: () => undo() } })

root_cause: React Hook Form provides performant form handling with minimal re-renders and Zod integration
fix: Install: npm install react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers zod
Setup: const { register, handleSubmit, formState: { errors } } = useForm<FormData>({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) })

Input: {errors.email &&

{errors.email.message}

}
Submit: <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(async (data) => { const result = await serverAction(data) })}>
Benefits: uncontrolled by default (no re-render per keystroke), validation on blur/submit, tiny bundle
With Server Actions: handleSubmit validates client-side first, then calls server action with typed data

root_cause: Proper image strategy can reduce page weight by 50%+ and dramatically improve LCP
fix: Always use next/image: auto-serves WebP/AVIF, resizes on-demand, lazy loads
Set priority on LCP image: <Image priority src={hero} /> (preloads, skips lazy loading)
Use sizes prop for responsive: sizes='(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw'
Placeholder blur for perceived performance: placeholder='blur' blurDataURL={base64}
Remote images: configure remotePatterns in next.config.js, not domains (deprecated)
Icon/SVG: import directly as component (not through next/image): import Logo from './logo.svg'
Quality: default 75 is good for most; use quality={90} only for hero/product images

root_cause: Optimized font loading prevents layout shift and flash of unstyled text
fix: Use next/font for automatic optimization: import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'
Apply: const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'] }); <body className={inter.className}>
Variable fonts: Inter({ subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-inter' }) → use in Tailwind config
Local fonts: import localFont from 'next/font/local'; const myFont = localFont({ src: './fonts/MyFont.woff2' })
Benefits: self-hosted (no Google Fonts request), preloaded, no CLS, no FOUT
In Tailwind config: fontFamily: { sans: ['var(--font-inter)', ...defaultTheme.fontFamily.sans] }

root_cause: Transactions ensure multiple database operations succeed or fail together
fix: Sequential: const [post, activity] = await prisma.$transaction([prisma.post.create({data}), prisma.activity.create({data})])

Interactive: await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const post = await tx.post.create({data}); await tx.notification.create({ data: { postId: post.id } }); if (someCondition) throw new Error('Rollback!') })
Set timeout: prisma.$transaction(fn, { maxWait: 5000, timeout: 10000 })
Use interactive transactions for: conditional logic, reading then writing, complex operations
Use batch transactions for: simple multi-model creates/updates without conditions
Don't hold transactions open too long (blocks other operations on locked rows)

root_cause: Proper API versioning prevents breaking existing clients when evolving your API
fix: URL-based versioning: /api/v1/users, /api/v2/users (most common, clearest)
In Next.js: app/api/v1/users/route.ts and app/api/v2/users/route.ts
Share logic: both versions call same service layer, differ in response mapping
Deprecation flow: v1 returns Deprecation header → remove after migration period
Non-breaking changes (safe): adding new fields, adding new endpoints
Breaking changes (need new version): removing fields, changing field types, changing response structure
For internal APIs (SPA): versioning often unnecessary since you control both sides

root_cause: Strategic code splitting ensures users only download code they actually need
fix: Heavy libraries: use dynamic import: const Chart = dynamic(() => import('./Chart'), { ssr: false })
Below the fold: lazy load components not visible on initial viewport
Route-based: Next.js automatically splits by route (each page is a chunk)
Conditional features: dynamic(() => import('./AdminPanel')) only loads for admin users
Named exports: dynamic(() => import('./utils').then(m => m.SpecificExport))
Loading state: dynamic(import, { loading: () => <Skeleton /> })
Analyze: npm install @next/bundle-analyzer to visualize what's in each chunk

root_cause: Well-crafted error messages reduce support requests and help users self-recover
fix: User-facing: say what happened + what they can do (not technical details)
Good: 'Unable to save your changes. Please check your connection and try again.'
Bad: 'Error: ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:5432 - Connection refused'
Developer logs: include full technical details, stack trace, request context
Validation errors: be specific per field: 'Email must be a valid email address'
Never expose: database errors, file paths, internal URLs, stack traces to users
Include error codes for support: 'Error E1042 - if this persists, contact support with this code'

root_cause: Regular dependency updates prevent security vulnerabilities and reduce migration pain
fix: Weekly: run npm audit and fix critical/high vulnerabilities immediately
Monthly: update patch/minor versions: npm update (respects semver ranges)
Quarterly: update major versions carefully: read changelogs, test thoroughly
Use Renovate/Dependabot for automated update PRs with changelog summaries
Pin critical dependencies: stripe, prisma, next (major version bumps need attention)
Test after updates: run full test suite, check build, verify critical flows
Don't fear updates: small frequent updates are safer than rare large jumps

root_cause: Race conditions in React cause stale data display when responses arrive out of order
fix: AbortController pattern: useEffect(() => { const controller = new AbortController(); fetch(url, { signal: controller.signal }).then(setData); return () => controller.abort() }, [url])
Stale closure fix: use useRef for latest value in async callbacks
With async state: track request ID, ignore responses from outdated requests
Cleanup pattern: let cancelled = false; fetchData().then(d => { if (!cancelled) setData(d) }); return () => { cancelled = true }
TanStack Query handles this automatically (built-in request cancellation)
Server Components avoid this entirely (no client-side fetching race conditions)

root_cause: Complete dark mode implementation with system preference detection and manual toggle
fix: Install: npm install next-themes
Provider (in layout): import { ThemeProvider } from 'next-themes'; <ThemeProvider attribute='class' defaultTheme='system' enableSystem>{children}</ThemeProvider>
Toggle button: import { useTheme } from 'next-themes'; const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme(); <button onClick={() => setTheme(theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}>
Tailwind config: darkMode: 'class' (already default in modern setups)
Apply dark styles: className='bg-white dark:bg-gray-900 text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-100'
Suppress hydration warning on html element: <html suppressHydrationWarning>
System detection: defaultTheme='system' respects OS preference automatically

root_cause: Strict TypeScript configuration catches more bugs at compile time
fix: Essential strict options: strict: true (enables all strict checks at once)
Additional recommended: noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true (arrays/objects may be undefined)
forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true (prevents issues on case-insensitive OS)
noImplicitReturns: true (all code paths must return or throw)
exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true (distinguishes missing from explicitly undefined)
Use skipLibCheck: true for faster builds (skip checking node_modules .d.ts files)
Next.js projects: use the tsconfig.json generated by create-next-app as base

root_cause: Server Components enable new data flow patterns that avoid prop drilling and API routes
fix: Direct DB access: async function Page() { const posts = await prisma.post.findMany(); return <PostList posts={posts} /> }
No API route needed: Server Components can directly call database, APIs, file system
Pass to client components as props: <InteractiveWidget data={serverData} />
For deeply nested data: fetch at the component that needs it (React deduplicates)
Don't prop-drill through many levels: each Server Component can fetch its own data
Shared data: use React cache() to deduplicate identical fetches across components
Sensitive operations stay on server: client never sees database queries or API keys

symptoms: when to use revalidatePath vs revalidateTag
root_cause: Choose the right revalidation granularity to balance freshness and performance
fix: revalidatePath('/posts'): invalidates all cached data for that specific page
revalidateTag('posts'): invalidates all fetch() calls tagged with 'posts' across any page
Tags are more surgical: one data source can affect multiple pages without over-invalidating
Use tags for: data that appears on multiple pages (user profile, products, settings)
Use path for: single-page revalidation after page-specific mutation
Call in Server Actions after successful mutation: revalidateTag('posts') after create/update/delete

root_cause: Deep prop drilling makes code fragile and hard to maintain
fix: Server Components: fetch data where it's needed (no need to pass from parent)
Context: for truly global state (theme, auth, locale) that many components need
Composition: pass components as children or slots instead of drilling data
Zustand: for client-side shared state that updates frequently
Bad: Page → Layout → Sidebar → Menu → MenuItem (passing user through all)
Good: MenuItem fetches user directly (Server Component) or uses useUser() hook (Client)

root_cause: Complete multi-provider OAuth setup with consistent UI and account linking
fix: Configure providers in auth config: providers: [GoogleProvider({...}), GitHubProvider({...})]
Login buttons: <button onClick={() => signIn('google')}>Continue with Google</button>
Account linking: when user logs in with different provider but same email, link accounts
With Prisma adapter: accounts table stores tokens per provider per user
Handle account conflicts: if email exists with different provider, show 'Account exists with Google'
UI pattern: show provider icons, loading state per button, error messages

root_cause: Typing indicators use Supabase Broadcast for ephemeral events without database writes
fix: Create channel: const channel = supabase.channel('chat:room-123')
Send typing event: channel.send({ type: 'broadcast', event: 'typing', payload: { userId, isTyping: true } })
Listen: channel.on('broadcast', { event: 'typing' }, ({ payload }) => { setTypingUsers(prev => payload.isTyping ? [...prev, payload.userId] : prev.filter(id => id !== payload.userId)) })
Debounce: only send 'typing: true' on first keystroke, 'typing: false' after 2s idle
Display: 'John is typing...' or 'John, Jane are typing...'
No database needed: broadcast events are ephemeral (fire-and-forget)

root_cause: Generate breadcrumbs dynamically from the current route path
fix: Hook: const pathname = usePathname(); const segments = pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
Generate crumbs: const breadcrumbs = segments.map((segment, index) => ({ label: segment.replace(/-/g, ' '), href: '/' + segments.slice(0, index + 1).join('/') }))

Render:
    {breadcrumbs.map(b =>
  1. {b.label}
    2. )}
For dynamic routes: replace [slug] with actual title from database or page metadata
Accessibility: use aria-label='Breadcrumb', aria-current='page' on current item
Structured data: add JSON-LD BreadcrumbList schema for SEO

root_cause: Server Actions are public HTTP endpoints - always verify authorization
fix: ALWAYS check auth: const session = await auth(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized')
Verify ownership: const post = await prisma.post.findUnique({where:{id}}); if (post.userId !== session.user.id) throw new Error('Forbidden')
Rate limit: apply rate limiting to prevent abuse (especially public-facing actions)
Validate input: const data = schema.parse(rawInput) before any database operation
Don't trust hidden form fields for authorization (IDs, user roles, permissions)
Treat server actions like API routes: they're callable by anyone who knows the endpoint
Never return sensitive data: filter response before sending back to client

symptoms: implement infinite scroll with Server Actions
root_cause: Load more pattern using Server Actions for server-rendered pagination without API routes
fix: Server Action: 'use server'; export async function loadMorePosts(cursor: string | null) { return prisma.post.findMany({ take: 20, ...(cursor ? { skip: 1, cursor: { id: cursor } } : {}), orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } }) }
Client component: const [posts, setPosts] = useState(initialPosts); const [cursor, setCursor] = useState(initialPosts.at(-1)?.id)
Load more: const newPosts = await loadMorePosts(cursor); setPosts(prev => [...prev, ...newPosts]); setCursor(newPosts.at(-1)?.id)
IntersectionObserver: detect when sentinel element is visible, trigger load
Disable when no more: if (newPosts.length < 20) setHasMore(false)
Benefits: no API route needed, type-safe, server-rendered content

symptoms: Supabase auth session lost on server component
root_cause: Server component can't access Supabase session without proper cookie forwarding
fix: Use createServerClient from @supabase/ssr (not createClient from @supabase/supabase-js)
Pass cookies: const supabase = createServerClient(url, key, { cookies: { getAll() { return cookieStore.getAll() }, setAll(cookies) { cookies.forEach(c => cookieStore.set(c)) } } })
In Server Component: const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser() (NOT getSession - getUser validates the JWT)
Middleware must refresh session: call supabase.auth.getUser() in middleware to refresh expired tokens
Without middleware refresh: tokens expire silently and server components get null user

root_cause: Slow builds increase deployment time and reduce developer productivity
fix: Enable Turbopack for dev: next dev --turbopack (not yet stable for build)
Use standalone output: output: 'standalone' in next.config.js (smaller deployment)
Reduce pages: pages with generateStaticParams generate at build time - limit count
Parallel routes: independent routes build in parallel automatically
Skip type checking in CI if done separately: typescript: { ignoreBuildErrors: true } (only if tsc runs in earlier CI step)
Optimize imports: use modularizeImports in next.config.js for barrel file tree-shaking
Remote caching: Vercel caches build artifacts automatically; local: use Turborepo

root_cause: Copy to clipboard with fallback for older browsers and user feedback
fix: Modern: await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text); toast.success('Copied!')

Component: function CopyButton({ text }: { text: string }) { const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); return <button onClick={async () => { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text); setCopied(true); setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000) }}>{copied ? 'Copied!' : 'Copy'}</button> }
Fallback for HTTP (clipboard API requires HTTPS): create temporary textarea, select, execCommand('copy')
Tailwind styling: className={cn('transition-colors', copied ? 'text-green-500' : 'text-gray-500')}
Accessibility: add aria-label='Copy to clipboard' and announce with aria-live

root_cause: Proper connection management prevents pool exhaustion and connection leaks in serverless
fix: Singleton pattern: declare const prisma: PrismaClient in global scope, reuse across warm invocations
Pool size: for serverless, keep small (2-5 connections per function instance)
Use pooled connection for queries (port 6543/pgbouncer), direct for migrations
Monitor: track connection count in Neon/Supabase dashboard, alert if approaching limit
Neon Pro: enable auto-scaling for connection limits under load
Close connections in tests: afterAll(() => prisma.$disconnect())
Never create new PrismaClient() per request - this leaks connections rapidly

root_cause: Command palette (Cmd+K) provides quick access to navigation, actions, and search
fix: Use cmdk library: npm install cmdk

Create component: import { Command } from 'cmdk'; <Command.Dialog open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}><Command.Input /><Command.List><Command.Group heading='Pages'><Command.Item onSelect={() => router.push('/dashboard')}>Dashboard</Command.Item></Command.Group></Command.List></Command.Dialog>

Keyboard shortcut: useEffect(() => { const down = (e) => { if (e.key === 'k' && (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) { e.preventDefault(); setOpen(true) } }; document.addEventListener('keydown', down); return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', down) }, [])
Search API: debounce input → fetch from /api/search → render results in Command.List
Group results: Pages, Actions (create post, toggle theme), Recent items
Tailwind styling: fixed inset-0 z-50, backdrop-blur-sm for overlay

root_cause: Prisma event logging helps identify slow queries and N+1 problems
fix: Enable query logging: new PrismaClient({ log: [{ level: 'query', emit: 'event' }] })
Listen: prisma.$on('query', (e) => { if (e.duration > 100) console.warn(`Slow query (${e.duration}ms): ${e.query}`) })

Middleware: prisma.$use(async (params, next) => { const start = Date.now(); const result = await next(params); const duration = Date.now() - start; if (duration > 200) console.warn(`Slow ${params.model}.${params.action}: ${duration}ms`); return result })
In development: log all queries to identify N+1 patterns
In production: only log slow queries (>100ms) to avoid log noise
Use prisma.$metrics for aggregated query statistics

root_cause: Optimistic delete provides instant feedback with undo capability before permanent deletion
fix: Remove from UI immediately: setItems(prev => prev.filter(i => i.id !== id))
Show undo toast: toast('Item deleted', { action: { label: 'Undo', onClick: () => undoDelete(id) }, duration: 5000 })
Schedule actual deletion: setTimeout(() => actuallyDelete(id), 5000)
Undo: re-add to UI state + cancel the scheduled deletion
Server: use soft delete (set deletedAt) then hard delete via cron after grace period
Pattern: optimistic UI → toast with undo → background permanent delete after timeout

root_cause: Serve different images at different breakpoints for optimal mobile experience
fix: Use sizes prop: <Image sizes='(max-width: 640px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw' />
This tells the browser which image width to download at each viewport size
For art direction (different crops): use <picture> with media queries
Aspect ratios: className='aspect-video object-cover' or 'aspect-square object-contain'
Mobile: show square crop; Desktop: show full landscape (use different src per breakpoint)
Lazy load all images except hero/LCP: next/image does this by default

root_cause: Warm critical cache entries after deployment to prevent cold cache latency spikes
fix: Create warming endpoint: app/api/cache-warm/route.ts
Warm popular data: await Promise.all([cacheTopProducts(), cacheNavigation(), cacheFeatureFlags()])
Trigger after deploy: use Vercel Deploy Hook or QStash scheduled message
With QStash: await qstash.publish({ url: `${APP_URL}/api/cache-warm`, delay: 5 }) // 5s after deploy
Prioritize: only warm data that's expensive to compute AND frequently accessed
Don't over-warm: warming everything wastes resources and may expire before being used

root_cause: next/link prefetch not working due to configuration or visibility issues
fix: Prefetch is enabled by default for <Link> components visible in viewport
In production only: prefetch doesn't work in development mode
Verify: links must be in viewport (IntersectionObserver triggers prefetch)
Manual prefetch: router.prefetch('/target-page') for programmatic preloading
Disable if causing too many requests: <Link prefetch={false} href='/heavy-page'>
Static pages prefetch fully; dynamic pages prefetch up to the loading.tsx boundary
Slow transitions may indicate expensive Server Component rendering (not prefetch issue)

root_cause: Feature flags enable safe deployment of incomplete features and gradual rollouts
fix: Store flags: Redis hash (fast reads), Vercel Edge Config (edge-fast), or dedicated service (LaunchDarkly)
Types: boolean (on/off), percentage (rollout %), user-targeting (specific users/groups)
Check pattern: if (await isFeatureEnabled('new-checkout', userId)) { renderNewCheckout() }
Implementation: const flags = await redis.hgetall('features'); const enabled = flags['new-checkout'] === 'true' || (parseFloat(flags['new-checkout']) > Math.random())
Kill switch: set flag to 'false' to instantly disable without redeploy
Cleanup: remove flag checks after full rollout (tech debt if left permanently)

root_cause: Debounced search reduces API calls while providing responsive search-as-you-type UX
fix: Custom hook: function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T { const [debounced, setDebounced] = useState(value); useEffect(() => { const timer = setTimeout(() => setDebounced(value), delay); return () => clearTimeout(timer) }, [value, delay]); return debounced }
Usage: const [search, setSearch] = useState(''); const debouncedSearch = useDebounce(search, 300); useEffect(() => { if (debouncedSearch) fetchResults(debouncedSearch) }, [debouncedSearch])
With URL sync: const searchParams = useSearchParams(); const router = useRouter(); useEffect(() => { router.push(`?q=${debouncedSearch}`) }, [debouncedSearch])
Cancel on unmount: AbortController in fetch to cancel stale requests
Show loading state during debounce delay + fetch time
300ms debounce is good default; adjust based on API speed and UX feel

root_cause: Route segment configuration controls rendering behavior at the page/layout level
fix: export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' | 'force-static' | 'auto' — control rendering strategy
export const revalidate = 3600 — ISR: revalidate every hour
export const maxDuration = 60 — extend serverless function timeout (Pro plan)
export const preferredRegion = 'sin1' — deploy function to specific region
export const runtime = 'edge' | 'nodejs' — choose execution runtime
Apply to page.tsx for single page, layout.tsx to affect all children
Check build output: next build shows (static), (dynamic), (ISR) per route

root_cause: Ensure webhook delivery to external services with automatic retries and monitoring
fix: Instead of direct HTTP call: queue via QStash for guaranteed delivery
Publish: await qstash.publishJSON({ url: webhookUrl, body: payload, retries: 5, callback: `${APP_URL}/api/webhook-status`, failureCallback: `${APP_URL}/api/webhook-failed` })
The target receives your payload; QStash retries on non-2xx with exponential backoff
Track delivery: callback endpoint receives success/failure notification from QStash
Store status in DB: webhooks table with status (pending/delivered/failed), attempts, lastError
Dashboard monitoring: Upstash Console shows delivery history and DLQ

symptoms: implement data table with sorting and filtering
root_cause: Server-side data table with URL-synced sorting, filtering, and pagination
fix: Use searchParams for state: ?page=2&sort=name&order=asc&status=active
Server Component reads params: const { page, sort, order, ...filters } = searchParams
Query with params: prisma.user.findMany({ where: filters, orderBy: { [sort]: order }, skip: (page-1)*20, take: 20 })
Column headers as Links: <Link href={`?sort=name&order=${toggleOrder}`}>Name ↕</Link>
Filter controls: use form with action that navigates to filtered URL
Benefits: URL-shareable state, works without JS, server-rendered, SEO-friendly
For client-side: use TanStack Table for complex interactions (drag, resize, virtual scroll)

root_cause: Appropriate loading and empty state patterns improve perceived performance and usability
fix: Loading: use skeleton screens that match content shape (not generic spinners)
Empty state: show helpful message + call-to-action ('No posts yet. Create your first post')
Error state: show what went wrong + how to fix/retry
Skeleton: className='animate-pulse bg-gray-200 rounded h-4 w-3/4' matching final content dimensions
loading.tsx: create per route segment for automatic Suspense wrapper
Never show infinite loading: add timeout → show error state if data never arrives
Partial loading: stream in sections independently with multiple Suspense boundaries

root_cause: Protect expensive AI endpoints from abuse with per-user rate limiting
fix: Combine auth + rate limiting: const session = await auth(); if (!session) return 401
Rate limit by user: const { success, remaining } = await ratelimit.limit(`ai:${session.user.id}`)
Different limits by plan: const limit = session.user.plan === 'pro' ? 100 : 10; // per hour
Track usage for billing: await redis.hincrby(`usage:${userId}:${month}`, 'ai_tokens', tokensUsed)
Cost control: set hard daily limit, alert when approaching: if (dailyTokens > 100000) return 429
Return remaining in headers: X-AI-Remaining: remaining, X-AI-Reset: resetTime

root_cause: Next.js special file routing patterns for flexible URL handling
fix: Catch-all: app/docs/[...slug]/page.tsx matches /docs/a, /docs/a/b, /docs/a/b/c
Optional catch-all: app/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx also matches /docs (no slug)
Access params: params.slug is string[] — e.g., ['a', 'b', 'c'] for /docs/a/b/c
Route groups: app/(marketing)/page.tsx and app/(app)/page.tsx — separate layouts, same URL
Parallel routes: app/@modal/(.)photo/[id] — intercepts /photo/[id] as modal
Use catch-all for: docs, CMS pages, multi-level categories
Generate static paths: export function generateStaticParams() { return slugs.map(s => ({ slug: s.split('/') })) }

root_cause: Redis stores strings - objects must be serialized/deserialized properly
fix: Upstash Redis automatically serializes/deserializes JSON when using .set() and .get()
For non-Upstash: always JSON.stringify before set, JSON.parse after get
Type-safe get: const data = await redis.get<MyType>(key) // Upstash auto-deserializes
Handle null: const cached = await redis.get(key); if (cached === null) { /* cache miss */ }
Date objects: JSON doesn't preserve Date - store as ISO string, parse on retrieval
Circular references: JSON.stringify fails - remove circular refs or use flatted library
Max value size: Upstash supports up to 1MB per value; split larger objects

root_cause: Store user preferences in database with Redis cache for fast reads
fix: Database model: model UserPreferences { userId String @id, theme String @default('system'), language String @default('en'), notifications Json @default('{}'), updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt }

Read (cache-first): async function getPreferences(userId) { const cached = await redis.get(`prefs:${userId}`); if (cached) return cached; const prefs = await prisma.userPreferences.findUnique({ where: { userId } }); await redis.set(`prefs:${userId}`, prefs, { ex: 3600 }); return prefs }
Write (invalidate): async function updatePreferences(userId, data) { await prisma.userPreferences.update({ where: { userId }, data }); await redis.del(`prefs:${userId}`) }
UI: settings form with server action, optimistic toggle for boolean settings
Apply: read in layout.tsx, set theme/locale accordingly

symptoms: Vercel Blob vs S3 vs Supabase Storage
root_cause: Choose the right file storage based on features, pricing, and integration needs
fix: Vercel Blob: simplest for Vercel apps, good for images/documents, max 500MB/file (Pro), automatic CDN
S3/R2 (Cloudflare): most flexible, cheapest at scale, requires presigned URLs for direct upload
Supabase Storage: best if already using Supabase, RLS-based access control, integrated auth
Decision: small app with Vercel → Vercel Blob; need RLS/auth → Supabase; high volume/custom → S3/R2
All support: direct upload from client, signed URLs for private files, CDN delivery
Cost: Vercel Blob included in plan; Supabase 1GB free; S3/R2 pay per storage+transfer

root_cause: Health check endpoint for monitoring services and load balancer health checks
fix: Create app/api/health/route.ts: export async function GET() { try { await prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT 1`; const redisOk = await redis.ping() === 'PONG'; return Response.json({ status: 'healthy', timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), services: { database: 'up', redis: redisOk ? 'up' : 'down' } }) } catch (e) { return Response.json({ status: 'unhealthy', error: e.message }, { status: 503 }) } }
Check DB connectivity, Redis connectivity, external service availability
Don't include sensitive details in response (no connection strings, versions)
Use for: uptime monitoring (Betterstack, Pingdom), load balancer health checks
Keep fast: timeout at 5s, don't run expensive queries
Add /api/health to monitoring service with 1-minute check interval

root_cause: const assertions create readonly literal types for better type inference
fix: Config objects: const ROLES = ['admin', 'user', 'moderator'] as const; type Role = typeof ROLES[number] // 'admin' | 'user' | 'moderator'
Route definitions: const routes = { home: '/', dashboard: '/dashboard', settings: '/settings' } as const
Status maps: const STATUS = { PENDING: 'pending', ACTIVE: 'active', CLOSED: 'closed' } as const
Without as const: type would be string[] — loses literal type information
Use with satisfies: const config = { port: 3000, host: 'localhost' } as const satisfies Config
Enum alternative: as const objects are more flexible than TypeScript enums (tree-shake, extend)

root_cause: Consistent API responses make client code predictable and error handling uniform
fix: Define standard shape: type ApiResponse<T> = { success: boolean; data?: T; error?: { code: string; message: string; details?: unknown } }
Success: return Response.json({ success: true, data: result })
Error: return Response.json({ success: false, error: { code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message: 'Invalid email' } }, { status: 400 })
Paginated: { success: true, data: items, meta: { total, page, pageSize, hasMore } }
Always include HTTP status code matching the response: 200, 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 500
Create helper: function apiSuccess<T>(data: T, status = 200) and apiError(code, message, status)

root_cause: Complete Stripe webhook handler for subscription lifecycle management
fix: Create app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts with raw body parsing
Verify: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(await request.text(), request.headers.get('stripe-signature')!, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!)

Handle key events: switch(event.type) { case 'checkout.session.completed': provision access; case 'invoice.payment_succeeded': extend subscription; case 'customer.subscription.deleted': revoke access }
Update database: await prisma.user.update({ where: { stripeCustomerId: event.data.object.customer }, data: { plan: 'pro', subscriptionStatus: 'active' } })
Return 200 immediately: Stripe retries on non-2xx for up to 3 days
Idempotency: store event.id in Redis/DB, skip if already processed
Test locally: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe

root_cause: Run Supabase locally for fast development without depending on cloud instance
fix: Install: npm install -D supabase
Init: npx supabase init (one-time, creates supabase/ directory)
Start: npx supabase start (launches local Postgres, Auth, Storage, Realtime via Docker)
Local credentials: supabase start prints local URL and anon key — use in .env.local
Migrations: write SQL in supabase/migrations/, apply with supabase db reset
Seed data: create supabase/seed.sql for development data
Benefits: offline development, faster iteration, no cloud usage/costs, isolated testing

root_cause: Exponential backoff prevents overwhelming failing services while ensuring eventual recovery
fix: async function withRetry<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, maxRetries = 3, baseDelay = 1000): Promise<T> { for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) { try { return await fn() } catch (error) { if (attempt === maxRetries) throw error; const delay = baseDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt) + Math.random() * 1000; await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, delay)) } } throw new Error('unreachable') }
Usage: const data = await withRetry(() => fetch(url).then(r => { if (!r.ok) throw new Error(r.statusText); return r.json() }))
Only retry transient errors (5xx, network errors); don't retry 4xx (client errors)
Add jitter (random component) to prevent thundering herd
Track retries in Redis for monitoring: redis.incr(`retries:${endpoint}:${hour}`)

symptoms: implement webhook signature for outgoing webhooks
root_cause: Sign outgoing webhook payloads so receivers can verify authenticity
fix: Generate signature: const signature = crypto.createHmac('sha256', webhookSecret).update(JSON.stringify(payload)).digest('hex')

Send with signature header: await fetch(webhookUrl, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'X-Webhook-Signature': `sha256=${signature}`, 'X-Webhook-Timestamp': Date.now().toString() }, body: JSON.stringify(payload) })
Include timestamp to prevent replay attacks: sign `${timestamp}.${body}`
Per-subscriber secrets: each webhook subscriber gets unique secret (store in DB)
Document for consumers: provide verification code example in your API docs
Rotate secrets: support both current and previous secret during rotation window

root_cause: Store dates in UTC, convert to user timezone only for display
fix: Always store as UTC in database: use DateTime (Prisma) or timestamptz (PostgreSQL)
Server: all operations in UTC — comparison, filtering, sorting on UTC values
Display: convert to user timezone only at render time in client components
Get user timezone: Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone (from browser)
Format: new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, { timeZone: userTz, dateStyle: 'medium', timeStyle: 'short' }).format(date)
Libraries: date-fns (tree-shakeable) or dayjs (lightweight) for complex operations
Never store local times: daylight saving changes will cause bugs

root_cause: Most Next.js apps should stay monolithic; split only when teams and scale demand it
fix: Monolith (recommended for most): single Next.js app, route groups for organization
When to consider splitting: 50+ developers, independently deployable features needed, different tech stacks per team
Vercel monorepo: single repo with Turborepo, multiple Next.js apps deployed independently
Shared packages: put common UI/utils in packages/ directory, import across apps
Communication: API routes between services, or shared database with domain boundaries
Don't prematurely split: coordination overhead of micro-frontends outweighs benefits for small teams
If splitting: use Module Federation or Vercel Multi-Zones for route-based splitting

root_cause: Auto-generate table of contents from page headings with active state tracking
fix: Extract headings: const headings = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('h2, h3')).map(el => ({ id: el.id, text: el.textContent, level: parseInt(el.tagName[1]) }))

Scroll spy: const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => { entries.forEach(entry => { if (entry.isIntersecting) setActiveId(entry.target.id) }) }, { rootMargin: '-100px 0px -80% 0px' }); headings.forEach(h => observer.observe(document.getElementById(h.id)!))

Render:
{headings.map(h => {h.text})}
Add IDs to headings: in markdown renderer or manually: <h2 id='section-1'>
Smooth scroll: html { scroll-behavior: smooth } or className='scroll-smooth' on html

root_cause: Place error boundaries strategically to isolate failures without losing the entire page
fix: One global error boundary: app/global-error.tsx (catches root layout errors)
Per route segment: app/dashboard/error.tsx (catches dashboard-specific errors)
Per widget: wrap independent sections in Suspense + error boundary
Pattern: if sidebar fails, main content still renders (and vice versa)
Reset capability: error.tsx receives reset() function to retry rendering
Don't catch everything at root: specific boundaries give better UX and debugging
Log differently: critical errors (auth, data corruption) → page team; widget errors → component owner

root_cause: Mobile-first responsive design ensures core functionality works on all devices
fix: Design mobile layout first, enhance for larger screens with Tailwind breakpoints
Use min-width breakpoints (Tailwind default): sm:, md:, lg:, xl:, 2xl:
Test on real devices, not just browser resize (touch targets, viewport behavior)
Key breakpoints: 640px (sm), 768px (md), 1024px (lg), 1280px (xl)
Touch targets minimum 44x44px for mobile accessibility
Use container queries for component-level responsiveness (@container)

root_cause: Consistent branching strategy enables parallel work and clean deployments
fix: Main branches: main (production), develop (staging/preview)
Feature branches: feature/add-user-auth, feature/payment-integration
Fix branches: fix/login-redirect-loop, fix/memory-leak-api
Naming: lowercase, hyphens, descriptive: type/short-description
Flow: feature branch → PR to main → Vercel preview → review → merge → auto-deploy
Keep branches short-lived: merge within 1-3 days to avoid conflicts

root_cause: Multiple components fetching the same data wastes resources and creates inconsistency
fix: Server Components: React automatically deduplicates identical fetch() calls in same render
Use React cache() for non-fetch async functions: const getUser = cache(async (id) => {...})
Client-side: use TanStack Query with same queryKey for automatic deduplication
Redis: cache query results for expensive computations shared across requests
Anti-pattern: fetching user data in header, sidebar, AND main content separately
Fix: fetch once in layout, pass as props, or use cache() for each component to call safely

symptoms: implement OAuth token refresh in background
root_cause: Background token refresh keeps users authenticated without interrupting their session
fix: Store refresh_token in database (encrypted) during initial OAuth flow
In JWT callback: check if access_token expires within 5 minutes
If expiring: call provider token endpoint with refresh_token to get new access_token
Update database with new tokens: prisma.account.update({ data: { access_token: newToken, expires_at: newExpiry } })
If refresh fails (token revoked): set error flag, redirect to re-login on next request
For background jobs (Inngest/cron): read refresh_token from DB, refresh before API call
Token rotation: some providers issue new refresh_token on each use — always save latest

root_cause: satisfies validates type compatibility while preserving narrower literal types
fix: const config = { port: 3000, host: "localhost" } satisfies ServerConfig // preserves literal types
vs type annotation: const config: ServerConfig = {...} // widens to string/number
Use for: configuration objects, route maps, theme definitions
Catches typos: { prto: 3000 } satisfies ServerConfig // ERROR: prto not in ServerConfig
Preserves autocomplete: config.host // type is "localhost" not string
Combine with as const: const routes = {...} as const satisfies RouteMap

root_cause: Log all webhook events for debugging, replay, and audit purposes
fix: Create table: model WebhookEvent { id String @id @default(cuid()), source String, eventType String, payload Json, signature String?, status String @default("received"), processedAt DateTime?, error String?, createdAt DateTime @default(now()) }
On receive: const event = await prisma.webhookEvent.create({ data: { source: "stripe", eventType: body.type, payload: body, signature: headers["stripe-signature"] } })
After processing: await prisma.webhookEvent.update({ where: { id: event.id }, data: { status: "processed", processedAt: new Date() } })
On error: await prisma.webhookEvent.update({ where: { id: event.id }, data: { status: "failed", error: err.message } })
Replay: read payload from DB, reprocess through same handler
Cleanup: delete events older than 30 days via cron job

root_cause: Set spending limits to prevent unexpected charges from traffic spikes or abuse
fix: Vercel Dashboard > Settings > Billing > Spend Management: set monthly budget cap
Enable email alerts at 50%, 75%, 90% of budget
Monitor: function invocations, bandwidth, build minutes are main cost drivers
Rate limit public APIs to prevent abuse-driven cost spikes
Cache aggressively: cached responses dont count as function invocations
Use Edge Config for frequently read data (cheaper than function invocations)
Review usage monthly: Vercel Dashboard > Usage shows breakdown

root_cause: Cursor-based pagination is more efficient and consistent than offset-based for large datasets
fix: API: app/api/posts/route.ts?cursor=lastId&limit=20
Query: const posts = await prisma.post.findMany({ take: limit + 1, ...(cursor ? { skip: 1, cursor: { id: cursor } } : {}), orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })
hasMore check: const hasMore = posts.length > limit; if (hasMore) posts.pop()
Response: return Response.json({ data: posts, nextCursor: hasMore ? posts.at(-1)?.id : null, hasMore })
Benefits vs offset: consistent results when data changes, no skipped/duplicate items
Client: pass nextCursor as cursor param for next page request

root_cause: Production security requires multiple defense layers working together
fix: Layer 1 - Network: HTTPS (Vercel auto), security headers (CSP, HSTS), rate limiting
Layer 2 - Authentication: secure session cookies, token refresh, proper logout
Layer 3 - Authorization: check permissions in EVERY server action and API route
Layer 4 - Input: validate all inputs with Zod, sanitize HTML, escape SQL (Prisma handles)
Layer 5 - Data: encrypt sensitive fields, never expose internal IDs, paginate responses
Layer 6 - Dependencies: audit regularly, pin critical versions, use lockfile
Layer 7 - Monitoring: error tracking, unusual activity detection, access logs

root_cause: Multi-tag filtering UI with URL-synced state for shareable filtered views
fix: Store tags as array: tags String[] in Prisma model (PostgreSQL native array)
Filter query: prisma.post.findMany({ where: { tags: { hasEvery: selectedTags } } }) // all tags must match
Or haseSome for any-match: { tags: { hasSome: selectedTags } }
URL sync: ?tags=react,typescript → searchParams.get("tags")?.split(",")
UI: toggle buttons that add/remove tag from URL params via router.push
Index: CREATE INDEX idx_tags ON posts USING GIN (tags) for fast array queries
Display: tags.map(t => <Badge key={t} onClick={() => toggleTag(t)} active={selectedTags.includes(t)}>{t}</Badge>)

symptoms: implement protected API with API key
root_cause: API key authentication for machine-to-machine or public API access
fix: Generate keys: crypto.randomBytes(32).toString("hex") → store hashed in DB
Hash before storing: crypto.createHash("sha256").update(apiKey).digest("hex")
Verify: const hashedKey = hash(request.headers.get("x-api-key")); const keyRecord = await prisma.apiKey.findUnique({ where: { hashedKey } })
Include metadata: apiKey model has userId, name, permissions, rateLimit, lastUsedAt, expiresAt
Rate limit per key: const { success } = await ratelimit.limit(keyRecord.id)
Revoke: soft delete or set expiresAt to now; active check: where: { expiresAt: null OR gt: now }

root_cause: Avoid common mistakes with next/image that cause layout shift or poor performance
fix: Always set width+height or use fill (never omit dimensions - causes CLS)
With fill: parent must have position:relative and explicit dimensions
priority prop on LCP image (usually hero): preloads and skips lazy loading
sizes prop is CRITICAL for responsive: without it, browser downloads largest size
Don't use next/image for tiny icons/SVGs: import directly or use inline SVG
Remote images: add to remotePatterns (not deprecated domains array)
Quality 75 default is fine for most images; only increase for product/hero images

symptoms: implement user avatar with initials fallback
root_cause: Avatar component that shows image when available, initials when not
fix:
Component: function Avatar({ user }: { user: { name: string; image?: string } }) { if (user.image) return ; const initials = user.name.split(" ").map(n => n[0]).join("").toUpperCase().slice(0,2); return
{initials}
}
Color from name: generate consistent color from name hash for personalized backgrounds
Sizes: create size variants with cva: sm (32px), md (40px), lg (64px)
Loading state: show skeleton circle while image loads
Next/Image: configure remotePatterns for OAuth provider avatar URLs (Google, GitHub)

root_cause: Consistent event naming enables discoverability and proper routing
fix: Use noun/verb pattern: "user/created", "order/placed", "payment/failed", "email/sent"
Namespace by domain: "billing/invoice.created", "auth/session.expired"
Event data schema: always include actor (who), subject (what), timestamp, and context
Type definitions: type Events = { "user/created": { data: { userId: string; email: string } }; ... }
Pass to Inngest client: new Inngest({ id: "my-app", schemas: new EventSchemas().fromRecord<Events>() })
Benefits: TypeScript autocompletion on event names and payloads, searchable in dashboard

root_cause: Correct key usage prevents subtle bugs with component state and list rendering
fix: Never use array index as key for dynamic lists (breaks on reorder/insert/delete)
Use stable unique identifier: database ID, UUID, or meaningful unique field
If no ID exists: generate at data creation time (not render time!)
Keys must be stable across renders: same data → same key always
Use key to force remount: <Form key={editingId} /> resets form state when editing different item
Sibling keys must be unique within the same parent (globally unique not required)

root_cause: Auto-save form data as user types with visual save indicator
fix: Track dirty state: const [isDirty, setDirty] = useState(false); const [isSaving, setSaving] = useState(false)

Debounce save: useEffect(() => { if (!isDirty) return; const timer = setTimeout(async () => { setSaving(true); await saveData(formData); setSaving(false); setDirty(false) }, 1500); return () => clearTimeout(timer) }, [formData, isDirty])
Indicator: {isSaving ? "Saving..." : isDirty ? "Unsaved changes" : "All changes saved"}

Prevent navigation with unsaved: useEffect(() => { if (isDirty) { const handler = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); e.returnValue = "" }; window.addEventListener("beforeunload", handler); return () => window.removeEventListener("beforeunload", handler) } }, [isDirty])
Server Action: save to database with upsert pattern
Offline: queue saves in localStorage, sync when back online

root_cause: Well-organized Tailwind component library enables consistent UI across the application
fix: Structure: src/components/ui/ with Button, Input, Card, Badge, Dialog, etc.
Use CVA for variants: each component has size, variant, and color options
Create cn() utility: export function cn(...inputs) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)) }
Allow className override: all components accept className prop merged with cn()
Design tokens in tailwind.config: extend theme with brand colors, spacing, radii
Consider shadcn/ui: copy-paste components using Radix + Tailwind (own the code, no dependency)
Document with Storybook or create a /design-system page showing all components

symptoms: Next.js redirect in server component not working
root_cause: redirect() must be called outside try/catch blocks as it throws a NEXT_REDIRECT error internally
fix: redirect() works by throwing a special error that Next.js catches - dont catch it in try/catch
Wrong: try { redirect("/login") } catch (e) { // catches the redirect! }
Right: check condition first, then redirect outside try/catch: if (!session) redirect("/login")
Or re-throw NEXT_REDIRECT: catch (e) { if (e instanceof Error && e.message === "NEXT_REDIRECT") throw e; handleError(e) }
In Server Actions: redirect must be called outside the try/catch or after it
Alternative: return { redirect: "/login" } from action, handle in client component with router.push

root_cause: Well-organized components are easier to maintain, test, and reuse
fix: One component per file (except small helper components used only by parent)
Structure: imports → types → component → exports (no logic below export)
Separate concerns: Container (data fetching) vs Presentational (pure UI)
Colocate related files: Button.tsx, Button.test.tsx, Button.stories.tsx in same directory
Keep components under 150 lines - extract sub-components when growing larger
Name files after the component: UserProfile.tsx exports function UserProfile()
Hooks in separate file if reused: useUserProfile.ts next to UserProfile.tsx

root_cause: Structured analytics tracking with type-safe event definitions
fix: Define events: type AnalyticsEvent = { name: "page_view"; props: { path: string } } | { name: "button_click"; props: { id: string; label: string } } | { name: "purchase"; props: { amount: number; productId: string } }
Track function: function track<T extends AnalyticsEvent>(event: T) { if (typeof window !== "undefined") { // Send to analytics service: Vercel Analytics, Mixpanel, PostHog } }
Usage: track({ name: "button_click", props: { id: "cta-hero", label: "Get Started" } })
Server-side: track in Server Actions for mutation events (purchase, signup)
Buffer events in Redis for batch processing: await redis.lpush("analytics", JSON.stringify(event))
Flush via cron: read batch from Redis, send to analytics service in bulk

root_cause: Clean conditional rendering patterns for auth-dependent and role-based UI
fix: Server Component (recommended): const user = await getUser(); if (!user) return <LoginPrompt />; if (user.role !== "admin") return <Forbidden />; return <AdminPanel />
Client-side auth: use session from useSession() or Supabase auth state
Avoid flash: render nothing (null) until auth state is known, use loading.tsx
Role-based: create <RequireRole role="admin">{children}</RequireRole> wrapper component
Feature-gated: <Feature flag="new-dashboard">{newDashboard}</Feature> with Redis flags
Never hide security-critical UI client-only: always enforce server-side too

root_cause: Type-safe error handling prevents uncaught errors and improves error reporting
fix: Never throw generic Error: create typed errors: class NotFoundError extends Error { code = "NOT_FOUND" }
Result pattern: type Result<T, E = Error> = { ok: true; data: T } | { ok: false; error: E }
Server Action: return Result type instead of throwing: if (!found) return { ok: false, error: { code: "NOT_FOUND" } }
Client handles: const result = await action(); if (!result.ok) { toast.error(result.error.message); return }
Zod errors: schema.safeParse returns { success, data?, error? } - use this pattern
Exhaustive error handling: switch on error.code for specific handling per error type

root_cause: Social sharing with native Web Share API and fallback share links
fix: Native: if (navigator.share) await navigator.share({ title, text, url })
Fallback links: Twitter: https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=${encodeURIComponent(text)}&url=${url}
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=${url}
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=${url}
Copy link fallback: navigator.clipboard.writeText(url); toast("Link copied!")
Add OpenGraph meta tags for rich previews when shared

root_cause: Seed scripts provide consistent development data for faster onboarding and testing
fix: Prisma: add seed script in package.json: "prisma": { "seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts" }
Run: npx prisma db seed (auto-runs after prisma migrate reset)
Create realistic data: use @faker-js/faker for names, emails, text content
Include edge cases: empty strings, long text, special characters, unicode
Separate seed files: seed-users.ts, seed-posts.ts, seed-comments.ts for modularity
Idempotent: use upsert or check existence before creating to allow re-running safely

root_cause: Expensive synchronous operations in React components block rendering and user interaction
fix: Move heavy computation to Web Workers or server-side (Server Actions)
Use useDeferredValue for expensive derived state that can lag behind
Split large lists: virtualize with react-window or tanstack-virtual
Debounce expensive operations triggered by user input
Pattern: compute on server → pass result as props (free CPU for client)
Never: JSON.parse large strings, sort thousands of items, regex on long text in render

root_cause: Nested writes create/update related records in a single atomic transaction
fix: Create with relation: await prisma.post.create({ data: { title, content, author: { connect: { id: userId } }, tags: { create: [{ name: "react" }] }, comments: { create: [{ text: "First!", author: { connect: { id: userId } } }] } } })
Create or connect: categories: { connectOrCreate: { where: { name }, create: { name } } }
Update nested: await prisma.user.update({ where: { id }, data: { profile: { upsert: { create: profileData, update: profileData } } } })
All operations in nested write are transactional (all succeed or all rollback)
Use for: creating entities with their relations in one call (less code, atomic)

root_cause: Render user-submitted or CMS markdown content safely with syntax highlighting
fix: Install: npm install react-markdown remark-gfm rehype-highlight
Render: <ReactMarkdown remarkPlugins={[remarkGfm]} rehypePlugins={[rehypeHighlight]} className="prose dark:prose-invert">{content}</ReactMarkdown>
GFM adds: tables, strikethrough, task lists, autolinks
Syntax highlighting: rehype-highlight or rehype-prism-plus for code blocks
Sanitize: react-markdown is safe by default (doesn't execute scripts in HTML)
For MDX (with components): use @next/mdx or contentlayer for MDX file support
Style with Tailwind Typography: @tailwindcss/typography plugin + prose class

symptoms: prevent data loss on concurrent save
root_cause: Detect and handle concurrent edits gracefully when multiple users edit same resource
fix: Store version/updatedAt with record: include in form as hidden field
On save: compare version with DB: if (record.version !== submittedVersion) → conflict
Show conflict UI: "This was modified by someone else. View changes or overwrite?"
Show diff: display both versions, let user choose or merge
Prevent: add real-time awareness with Supabase Presence (show who is editing)
Alternative: auto-merge non-conflicting field changes (like Git merge for data)

root_cause: Middleware pattern for route handlers adds auth, logging, error handling consistently
fix: Create wrapper: function withAuth(handler: AuthenticatedHandler) { return async (req: NextRequest) => { const session = await auth(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 }); return handler(req, session) } }
Export: export const GET = withAuth(async (req, session) => { /* handler with guaranteed session */ })
Compose: export const POST = withAuth(withRateLimit(async (req, session) => { ... }))
Add logging: log request start/end, duration, status code
Add error boundary: try/catch with Sentry reporting and consistent error response
Benefits: DRY auth checks, consistent error responses, easy to add cross-cutting concerns

root_cause: Complete email verification flow for new user registration
fix: 1. On signup: generate token: crypto.randomBytes(32).toString("hex"); store hashed in DB with userId + expiresAt
2. Send email: link to /verify-email?token=xxx (use Resend or similar)
3. Verify endpoint: hash submitted token, find matching record, check not expired
4. Mark verified: await prisma.user.update({ where: { id }, data: { emailVerified: new Date() } })
5. Delete token: await prisma.verificationToken.delete({ where: { token: hashedToken } })
6. Redirect to success page or auto-login
Security: tokens expire (24h), one-time use (delete after verify), hash before storing

symptoms: when to use edge vs nodejs runtime
root_cause: Choose the right runtime based on performance needs and API compatibility
fix: Edge runtime: fast cold start (< 5ms), runs in 30+ regions, limited API surface
Node.js runtime: full Node.js API access, higher cold start, single region default
Use Edge for: simple redirects, auth checks, geo-routing, rate limiting, static responses
Use Node.js for: database queries (Prisma), file system access, heavy computation, most API routes
Edge limitations: no fs, limited crypto, no native modules, 1MB bundle limit, shorter timeout
Default to Node.js unless you specifically need edge speed/geo-distribution
Middleware always runs on edge: keep it lightweight and fast

root_cause: Webhook dispatch system for notifying external subscribers of events
fix: Store subscribers: model WebhookSubscription { id, url, events String[], secret, isActive, createdAt }
On event: const subscribers = await prisma.webhookSubscription.findMany({ where: { events: { has: eventType }, isActive: true } })

Dispatch via QStash (reliable): for (const sub of subscribers) { await qstash.publishJSON({ url: sub.url, body: { event: eventType, data: payload, timestamp: Date.now() }, headers: { "X-Webhook-Signature": sign(payload, sub.secret) }, retries: 3 }) }
Benefits of QStash: automatic retry, delivery tracking, no blocking your API
Track delivery: store message ID from QStash, check status via callback
Allow subscribers to manage via API: CRUD endpoints for webhook subscriptions

root_cause: Logs must never contain passwords, tokens, or personally identifiable information
fix: Never log: passwords, API keys, tokens, credit card numbers, full SSNs
Mask PII: log email as j***@example.com, phone as ***-***-1234
Use structured logging: JSON format with explicit fields (easy to filter/redact)
Redact in log middleware: before writing, scan for known sensitive patterns
Log IDs not data: log userId not userEmail, log orderId not order details
Retention: delete logs after 30-90 days (GDPR right to erasure compliance)
Access control: limit who can view production logs

root_cause: Secure password reset flow with time-limited tokens
fix: 1. User submits email on /forgot-password form
2. Generate token: crypto.randomBytes(32).toString("hex"); hash and store with userId + expiresAt (1 hour)
3. Send reset email with link: /reset-password?token=xxx (use Resend)
4. ALWAYS show "If account exists, email sent" (don't reveal if email is registered)
5. Reset page: verify token (hash submitted, find in DB, check expiry)
6. Update password: hash new password with bcrypt, delete token, invalidate all sessions
Security: rate limit requests (3/hour per IP), one-time tokens, short expiry (1h), log attempts

root_cause: Optimized Prisma queries prevent performance issues as data grows
fix: Use include for needed relations: prisma.post.findMany({ include: { author: true } }) instead of separate queries per post
Use select to limit fields: prisma.user.findMany({ select: { id: true, name: true } }) - less data transfer
Avoid N+1: if looping and querying per item, restructure to findMany with where: { id: { in: ids } }
Add @@index to Prisma schema for filtered/sorted columns
Use _count for aggregation: prisma.user.findMany({ include: { _count: { select: { posts: true } } } })
Paginate always: never findMany() without take limit on potentially large tables
Monitor with Prisma logging: log queries over 100ms in development

root_cause: Global keyboard shortcuts for power-user productivity features
fix: Hook: useEffect(() => { const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.metaKey && e.key === "k") { e.preventDefault(); openCommandPalette() } if (e.key === "Escape") closeModal() }; window.addEventListener("keydown", handler); return () => window.removeEventListener("keydown", handler) }, [])
Check for input focus: if (document.activeElement?.tagName === "INPUT") return (don't trigger during typing)
Common shortcuts: Cmd+K (search), Escape (close), Cmd+Enter (submit form), Cmd+S (save)
Display hints: show shortcut labels on buttons/menu items: ⌘K, ⌘S, Esc
Accessibility: shortcuts must not conflict with screen reader shortcuts
Library alternative: use react-hotkeys-hook for declarative hotkey binding

root_cause: Proactive tech debt management prevents codebase degradation over time
fix: Track debt: add TODO/FIXME comments with issue links, review quarterly
Boy Scout Rule: leave code better than you found it (small improvements per PR)
Refactor when: touching code anyway, before adding features to messy area, test coverage exists
Don't refactor: working code nobody touches, without tests, under deadline pressure
Budget: dedicate 10-20% of each sprint to tech debt reduction
Prioritize: debt that slows new features > debt that might cause bugs > cosmetic debt

root_cause: Use Inngest events to control feature rollout and quickly disable features
fix: Store flag: await redis.hset("features", { newCheckout: "0.1" }) // 10% rollout
Check: const percentage = parseFloat(await redis.hget("features", "newCheckout") || "0")
